title: Spatial Inequalities in PM2.5 Exposure and Income Inequality in California Counties (2019)
research_question: How do spatial inequalities in PM2.5 exposure across California counties vary with income inequality (Gini) in 2019, and does the spatial relationship vary by urban–rural classification?
research_intent: To understand the relationship between spatial inequalities in PM2.5 exposure and income inequality across California counties and investigate how this relationship varies by urban-rural classification.

objectives.title: Assess how PM2.5 exposure varies across California counties in 2019 and whether county-level PM2.5 is spatially clustered
objectives.hypothesis: County-level annual PM2.5 exposure in 2019 is spatially clustered (positive global Moran’s I) rather than randomly distributed across California.

data_requirements.description: PM2.5 monitoring-site annual summaries to be aggregated to county-level PM2.5 exposure using `state_code`, `county_code`, `year`, `pollutant_standard`, and `arithmetic_mean`.

scale_note.data_scale: monitoring site records; county identifiers present

data_preprocessing.rationale: The PM2.5 data are at monitoring-site record level; the research question requires county-level 2019 exposure.
data_preprocessing.method: Filter to `year == 2019` and `pollutant_standard == 'PM25 Annual 2006'`, derive county `GEOID` from `state_code` and `county_code`, then aggregate `arithmetic_mean` to county mean.

data_requirements.description: California county boundaries and identifiers (`GEOID`, `NAME`, `geometry`) for mapping and contiguity weights.

data_preprocessing.rationale: County PM2.5 summaries must be joined to county geometry to compute contiguity weights and spatial autocorrelation.

what_each_contributes.PM2.5_Annual_California_2019: monitoring-site `arithmetic_mean` values to summarize into county exposure

data_preprocessing.method: Left-join county PM2.5 summary to `california_counties_2019` on `GEOID`.

steps.description: Filter `PM2.5_Annual_California_2019` to rows where `year == 2019` and `pollutant_standard == 'PM25 Annual 2006'`, keeping only `state_code`, `county_code`, `site_number`, and `arithmetic_mean`.
Create county `GEOID` as a 5-character string by concatenating zero-padded `state_code` (2 digits) and zero-padded `county_code` (3 digits).
Group by `GEOID` and compute `pm25_mean_2019 = mean(arithmetic_mean)` and `pm25_site_count_2019 = count(site_number)`.
steps.expected_output: A county-level PM2.5 table with `GEOID`, `pm25_mean_2019`, and `pm25_site_count_2019`.

steps.description: Left-join `pm25_county_2019.csv` to `california_counties_2019` on `GEOID` to attach `pm25_mean_2019` and `pm25_site_count_2019` to county polygons.
Create `pm25_missing_2019` as a boolean indicator where `pm25_mean_2019` is null to identify counties with no monitor-derived PM2.5 summary in 2019.
Construct Queen contiguity spatial weights from county `geometry`, keyed by `GEOID`.
Compute global Moran’s I for `pm25_mean_2019` using the Queen contiguity weights.
steps.expected_output: A table with Moran’s I, expected I, variance, z-score, and p-value for `pm25_mean_2019` in 2019.

objectives.title: Estimate the county-level association between PM2.5 exposure and income inequality (Gini) in 2019 and test whether residual spatial autocorrelation remains
objectives.hypothesis: Higher county income inequality (`B19083_001E:Gini Index`) is associated with higher county mean PM2.5 (`pm25_mean_2019`) in 2019, and OLS residuals exhibit spatial autocorrelation indicating the need for a spatial regression specification.

data_requirements.description: County-level income inequality for 2019 using `state_fips`, `county_fips`, `year`, and `B19083_001E:Gini Index`.

data_preprocessing.rationale: Gini must be filtered to 2019 and joined to the county PM2.5 layer by county `GEOID`.
data_preprocessing.method: Filter Gini to `year == 2019`, derive `GEOID` from `state_fips` and `county_fips`, then left-join to the county PM2.5 layer on `GEOID`.

data_requirements.description: Derived county PM2.5 layer from Objective 1, used as a fixed input for Objective 2.

data_preprocessing.rationale: This is an upstream derived product.

data_requirements.description: Derived Queen contiguity weights from Objective 1, keyed by `GEOID`.

data_preprocessing.rationale: This is an upstream derived product.

data_requirements.description: Derived K-nearest-neighbors (KNN) spatial weights (k=5) for California counties in 2019, keyed by `GEOID`.

data_preprocessing.rationale: This is a within-objective derived product to support the SEM specification using KNN weights.

steps.description: Filter `California_Counties_Gini_2019` to rows where `year == 2019`, keeping only `state_fips`, `county_fips`, and `B19083_001E:Gini Index`.
Create county `GEOID` as a 5-character string by concatenating zero-padded `state_fips` (2 digits) and zero-padded `county_fips` (3 digits), and retain `GEOID` and `B19083_001E:Gini Index`.
Left-join `gini_2019_with_geoid.csv` to `counties_pm25_2019_qc.gpkg` on `GEOID` to attach `B19083_001E:Gini Index` to each county.
steps.expected_output: County polygons with `pm25_mean_2019` and `B19083_001E:Gini Index` for 2019.

steps.description: Fit an OLS model with dependent variable `pm25_mean_2019` and predictor `B19083_001E:Gini Index`, and append model outputs to features as `ols_fitted_pm25` and `ols_residual_pm25` keyed by `GEOID`.
steps.expected_output: County layer with OLS fitted values (`ols_fitted_pm25`) and residuals (`ols_residual_pm25`) for `pm25_mean_2019`.

steps.description: Compute global Moran’s I for `ols_residual_pm25` using the Queen contiguity weights keyed by `GEOID`.
steps.expected_output: A table with Moran’s I, z-score, and p-value for spatial autocorrelation in OLS residuals.

steps.description: Fit a maximum-likelihood spatial lag (SAR) model with dependent variable `pm25_mean_2019` and predictor `B19083_001E:Gini Index` using the Queen contiguity weights keyed by `GEOID`.
steps.expected_output: A table of spatial lag model estimates including the `B19083_001E:Gini Index` coefficient, the spatial autoregressive parameter, standard errors, and fit statistics.

steps.description: Construct K-nearest-neighbors (KNN) spatial weights with k=5 from county `geometry` centroids, keyed by `GEOID`.
Fit a maximum-likelihood spatial error (SEM) model with dependent variable `pm25_mean_2019` and predictor `B19083_001E:Gini Index` using KNN weights (k=5) keyed by `GEOID`.
steps.expected_output: A table of spatial error model estimates including the `B19083_001E:Gini Index` coefficient, the spatial error parameter, standard errors, and fit statistics.

objectives.title: Test whether the 2019 PM2.5–income inequality association differs by metro status at the county level
objectives.hypothesis: The association between `B19083_001E:Gini Index` and `pm25_mean_2019` differs by `Metro2013` status (effect modification).

data_requirements.description: County metro classification using `FIPSTXT` and `Metro2013` (with supporting fields `Nonmetro2013` and `RuralUrbanContinuumCode2013`).

scale_note.data_scale: county; metro codes are labeled as 2013-based

data_preprocessing.rationale: Metro classification must be joined to the county PM2.5+Gini layer using a county identifier; the classification is labeled 2013-based and should be treated as time-invariant for 2019 with this limitation documented.
data_preprocessing.method: Format `FIPSTXT` as 5-character county `GEOID` and left-join to the county PM2.5+Gini layer on `GEOID`.

data_requirements.description: Derived county PM2.5 + Gini layer from Objective 2, used as a fixed input for Objective 3.

data_preprocessing.rationale: This is an upstream derived product.

data_requirements.description: Derived Queen contiguity weights from Objective 1, keyed by `GEOID`.

data_preprocessing.rationale: This is an upstream derived product.

steps.description: Create county `GEOID` as a 5-character string from `FIPSTXT` using zero-padding, and keep `GEOID`, `Metro2013`, `Nonmetro2013`, and `RuralUrbanContinuumCode2013`.
Left-join `urban_rural_with_geoid.csv` to `counties_pm25_gini_2019.gpkg` on `GEOID` to attach `Metro2013` to each county.
Create interaction term `gini_x_metro = (B19083_001E:Gini Index) * Metro2013`.
Fit an OLS model with dependent variable `pm25_mean_2019` and predictors `B19083_001E:Gini Index`, `Metro2013`, and `gini_x_metro`, and report coefficient estimates, standard errors, and p-values.
steps.expected_output: A coefficient table where the `gini_x_metro` term tests whether the `B19083_001E:Gini Index` slope differs by `Metro2013`.

steps.description: Fit a maximum-likelihood spatial lag (SAR) model with dependent variable `pm25_mean_2019` and predictors `B19083_001E:Gini Index`, `Metro2013`, and `gini_x_metro` using Queen contiguity weights keyed by `GEOID`.
steps.expected_output: A spatial lag model coefficient table where `gini_x_metro` quantifies metro effect modification under spatial dependence.

steps.description: Compute Spearman rank correlation between `pm25_mean_2019` and `B19083_001E:Gini Index` separately for counties where `Metro2013 == 1` and where `Metro2013 == 0`, reporting correlation and group sample size.
steps.expected_output: A two-row table of metro vs nonmetro Spearman correlations with sample sizes.

issues_found: Cross-objective step connectivity was broken because Objectives 2 and 3 referenced outputs from Objective 1 (e.g., `counties_pm25_2019.gpkg`, `ca_county_queen_weights_2019.gpkg`) without those being resolvable as source_id inputs within those objectives.
Objective 2 attempted Moran’s I on OLS residuals without an explicit step that produces residuals keyed by `GEOID` for alignment with spatial weights.
Objective 1 county aggregation did not retain monitoring-site sample size per county, limiting interpretability and QA of county means (counties with few/zero monitors).
Objective 3 used groupwise Pearson correlation, which is sensitive to non-normality and outliers in small group sizes; a rank-based correlation is more robust for this setting.
Several intermediate-step expected_output fields were overly verbose for purely intermediate tables.
changes_made: Added derived-input data_requirements in Objectives 2 and 3 so that outputs from earlier objectives can be referenced as source_id values and pass step connectivity checks.
Revised Objective 2 to explicitly fit OLS while appending residuals back to county features (keyed by `GEOID`) before computing Moran’s I on residuals.
Enhanced Objective 1 county aggregation to compute both `pm25_mean_2019` and `pm25_site_count_2019`, and added a step to flag counties with missing PM2.5 due to no monitors.
Replaced Objective 3 groupwise Pearson correlation with groupwise Spearman rank correlation.
Tightened step descriptions to reference verified column names exactly and set expected_output to null for purely intermediate artifacts.
Added a maximum-likelihood spatial error (SEM) model in Objective 2 to complement the spatial lag specification.
